documents.title: Declaration Message of Independence
documents.description: A record about the declaration message that inspired people to join the struggle for independence.

documents.title: Proclamation of Independence
documents.description: A document explaining the formal political direction of the new Bangladesh government.

documents.title: Mujibnagar Government Note
documents.description: A note about the formation and activities of the first government of Bangladesh.

documents.title: Refugee Relief Report
documents.description: A report describing the condition of refugees and the need for food, shelter, and medical help.

documents.title: International Support Letter
documents.description: A letter showing support from international friends for the people of Bangladesh.

documents.title: Sector Command Report
documents.description: A simple report about freedom fighter activities in a war sector.

documents.title: Surrender Document
documents.description: A document related to the surrender of Pakistani forces in Dhaka.

documents.title: Post War Reconstruction Note
documents.description: A note about rebuilding the country after the Liberation War.

interviews.name: Abdul Karim
interviews.biography: Abdul Karim joined the war as a young student and served in a local freedom fighter group.
interviews.summary: He described training, courage, and the support of villagers during the war.

interviews.name: Shamsun Nahar
interviews.biography: Shamsun Nahar was a teenager in 1971 and saw many families leaving their homes.
interviews.summary: Her interview explains the fear, struggle, and hope of ordinary people.

interviews.name: Mizanur Rahman
interviews.biography: Mizanur Rahman helped collect food, medicine, and clothing for freedom fighters.
interviews.summary: He shared how local people supported the movement with whatever they had.

interviews.name: Rafiqul Islam
interviews.biography: Rafiqul Islam fought in several operations near border areas.
interviews.summary: His testimony talks about teamwork, discipline, and sacrifice.

interviews.name: Ayesha Begum
interviews.biography: Ayesha Begum lost family members during the war and preserved their memories.
interviews.summary: She spoke about family pain and the importance of remembering history.

interviews.name: Hasan Ali
interviews.biography: Hasan Ali joined student movements before the war started.
interviews.summary: He explained the role of students in building awareness among people.

interviews.name: Nurjahan Khatun
interviews.biography: Nurjahan Khatun worked as a volunteer and helped injured people.
interviews.summary: Her interview focuses on medical support and community service.

interviews.name: Abu Sayeed
interviews.biography: Abu Sayeed was a village resident who witnessed important events in 1971.
interviews.summary: He shared memories about village life during the war and the day of victory.
